title: Freshly Roasted Australian Lamb Roast
description: A hearty and flavorful Australian dish featuring roasted lamb, mitsuba, and a blend of earthy spices, perfect for a crowd-pleasing dinner. This recipe is onion and garlic free, making it suitable for those with dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb roast with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb roast on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes per pound, or until the internal temperature reaches 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
While the lamb is roasting, prepare the mitsuba by chopping it into small pieces and mixing it with lemon juice.
After the lamb has roasted for 30 minutes, add the chopped mitsuba and rosemary to the skillet.
Continue roasting for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked to desired doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let the lamb rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
